title: China deploys its first forest fire robot — tripling firebreak speed!
content: China has deployed its first remote-controlled robot for fire prevention, with the crawler-mounted machine tripling firebreak efficiency and eliminating the need for workers to operate near flames.

title: Wood Central
content: Wood Central is Australia’s first dedicated platform covering wood-based media across all digital platforms.

title: Cyclone Narelle: ‘compact’, dangerous and unusually predictable
content: Narelle is a very compact cyclone, meaning it has a relatively small area of intense winds around its centre. Due to the laws of physics, smaller cyclones typically spin up faster than larger ones.

title: 'Women are responsible for cooking': Japan disaster manuals show gender-biased burden
content: In Japan, it is not uncommon to see community disaster manuals stating, "Women are responsible for cooking" in times of such emergencies.

title: Mapping Malawi’s landscape restoration future
content: Landscape restoration is central to Malawi’s development ambitions. In 2016, Malawi pledged to restore 4.5 million hectares of degraded and deforested land by 2030 as part of the African Forest Landscape Restoration Initiative (AFR100).

title: National consultant – Development of localization model (Vientiane, Lao PDR)
content: The purpose of this consultancy is to facilitate the development and documentation of a country (i.e., LDPP)-specific Localization Model, capturing how localization has been defined, operationalized, and institutionalized through the partnership.

title: Chief of Section, P-5, New York Liaison Office
content: The position is responsible for the UNDRR New York Liaison Office and reports to the Chief of Intergovernmental Processes, Interagency Cooperation and Partnerships Branch, who is based in Geneva.

title: National Consultant – Development of Localization Model (Manila, Philippines)
content: ADPC is seeking a Manila-based national consultant to develop and document a localization model for disaster preparedness and climate resilience in the Philippines.

title: Reclaim Finance
content: Reclaim Finance is a non-governmental research and campaigning organization. It was founded in March 2020 by Lucie Pinson, winner of the 2020 Goldman Environmental Prize, with the aim of making finance work for the climate.

title: New model to forecast space weather on way
content: Aberystwyth scientists are developing a new model using coronagraph data to map the Sun’s magnetic field more accurately, improving forecasts of solar storms that threaten satellites, power grids and communications.

title: Bridging the last mile: Rethinking disaster risk communication in academic research
content: Risk communication is the bridge between science and preparedness. If academic research continues to prioritise prediction without equally focusing on how warnings are understood and acted upon, early warning systems will remain socially ineffective.

title: Integrated National Financing Framework (INFF)
content: The INFF Facility is a joint flagship initiative that responds to the demand for support from countries developing integrated national financing frameworks (INFFs)

title: Adapt or die: Why progressives need to deal with extreme weather
content: This report outlines how increasingly frequent and severe extreme weather events - flooding, storms, heatwaves and wildfires - are already undermining the UK’s public services, economic stability and political cohesion.

title: Failure to prepare for extreme weather risks losing a flood of public support, warns IPPR
content: The IPPR warns that failing to prepare for extreme weather is fuelling public anger and empowering populists. Most voters think the UK is unprepared, creating a mandate for policies that pair climate adaptation with emissions cuts.

title: Definitions and taxonomy for High Impact Low Probability (HILP) and outlier events
content: This study outlines a comprehensive effort to clarify and standardise how High Impact Low Probability (HILP) and outlier events are defined and categorised, with the aim of strengthening disaster risk reduction across sectors.

title: Validating disaster and climate resilience: how to create a gold standard for resilience-measurement
content: As climate risks intensify, fostering community resilience has become a global priority but a fundamental question remains for practice and policy: how can resilience be defined, measured, and proven to inform implementation?

title: Future Proof
content: Future Proof was born from the Paulele Hale Association (TPHA), a nonprofit founded to pioneer climate-resilient, affordable housing.

title: Emergencia Animal
content: Emergencia Animal's mission is to integrate animal welfare into disaster risk reduction frameworks across Spain, ensuring that families, animal shelters, and communities can effectively prepare for, respond to, and recover from emergencies.

title: Strengthening urban climate resilience: Socio-economic and institutional drivers among refugee populations in Lilongwe, Malawi
content: Urban refugees face compounded climate vulnerabilities that remain poorly understood.

title: World Intangible Cultural Heritage Federation (WICHF)
content: World Intangible Cultural Heritage Federation (WICHF) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to documenting, safeguarding and promoting intangible cultural heritage, traditional knowledge and community-based cultural practices.

title: Bahar Organization
content: Bahar is a local organization delivering support across northern Syria and in Iraq.

title: Assessing village and savings associations (VSLA’s) contribution to urban resilience: Evidence from Northern Ghana
content: This study explores the role of Village Savings and Loan Associations (VSLAs) in enhancing urban resilience to climate change in Northern Ghana, focusing on the cities of Tamale and Bolgatanga.

title: The United Nations World Water Development Report 2026: Water for all people: equal rights and opportunities
content: The United Nations World Water Development Report 2026: Water for All People – Equal Rights and Opportunities highlights how unequal access to water and sanitation services impacts health, education, livelihoods and safety.

title: What disaster preparedness experts have packed in their go bags
content: Climate change is making extreme weather events and natural hazards more frequent and intense. Here’s how to prepare before they hit.

title: Evaluating the multiplier effect of micro-insurance on climate resilience among motorcycle taxi drivers in Dar es Salaam
content: This study investigates the multiplier effect of micro-insurance on climate resilience and socio-economic stability among motorcycle taxi (boda-boda) drivers in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania.

title: Florida farmers face rising climate risks from extreme weather
content: In the last decade, extreme weather has become a greater economic liability for Florida farmers. In response, farmers are adopting practices designed to reduce long-term costs and improve resilience.

title: Socio-economic pathways to urban climate resilience: Exploring governance and incentives for equitable climate action in global south cities
content: This study investigates the socio-economic pathways to urban climate resilience in the cities of Mbabane and Manzini, Eswatini, focusing on the role of governance and socio-economic incentives in fostering equitable climate action.

title: The impact of climate risks on European sovereign credit ratings and long-term borrowing costs
content: This paper presents an empirical assessment of how climate‑related risks shape sovereign credit ratings and long‑term borrowing costs across the EU‑27 and the United Kingdom.

title: International Review of Economics & Finance
content: IREF publishes high-quality theoretical and empirical research across the broad fields of economics and finance.

title: More than a financing mechanism, REPAIR shares knowledge to strengthen preparedness for future shocks
content: Through REPAIR, Comorian authorities were able to request the activation of funds for emergency relief. The access to these funds allowed the Comoros’s operational and financial emergency response system to reach quickly.

title: Coverage or intensity of support? Adaptive social protection to reduce disaster-related poverty
content: This paper outlines a global assessment of how adaptive social protection can reduce disaster‑related poverty by analysing the balance between expanding coverage and increasing the intensity of support across 131 countries.

title: USA: Volcano hazards in Hawai‘i: managed risk and tailored recovery
content: Respecting shared history can enhance the effectiveness of emergency response and recovery. Acknowledging Hawaiian traditions in public education can improve community engagement by connecting scientific information with community identity.

title: The 2023 South Lhonak Glacial Lake Outburst Flood: Insights, lessons and recommendations
content: This brief on the key lessons from the Sikkim, India, 2023 glacial lake outburst flood highlights the importance of early warning systems, hazard assessments, and risk reduction management in the face of growing climate-related hazards.

title: Satellite data reconstructs 2025 LA fires, showing rapid spread in first day
content: Researchers in South Dakota State University's Geospatial Sciences Center of Excellence utilized satellite data to better understand how destructive wildfires swept through Los Angeles in January 2025.

title: Artificial Intelligence captures complex disaster risk
content: The next frontier of AI will not be just predicting a storm's path. It will be about understanding the "invisible dominoes"—the complex, compounding, and cascading risks that happen when our infrastructure fails.

title: In the wake of fire, how South Korea is climate-proofing its forests and communities
content: Achieving social and environmental goals simultaneously is at the heart of the ambitious effort underway to restore the forests of Uljin, which in 2022 suffered the country’s second-worst wildfire on record.

title: ‘Disaster inertia’: why must NZ keep relearning the same lessons from extreme events?
content: Our analysis of New Zealand’s post-disaster reviews over the past decade shows the same problems – some dating back to 1986 – have been repeatedly identified but rarely translated into meaningful policy reform.

title: African coastlines face unprecedented sea level surge crisis
content: A groundbreaking study led by researchers at the University of Cape Town’s (UCT) Department of Oceanography has revealed that Africa’s coastlines are facing a rapidly accelerating crisis.

title: Building climate-resilient agriculture in Europe: An economic perspective
content: This briefing links climate hazards to farm vulnerability and economic impacts and synthesises quantified findings from European farm-level case studies to demonstrate that climate-resilient agriculture is a economic imperative for Europe’s food security.

title: Slope stabilisation and modernisation of road infrastructure at Gródek nad Dunajcem, Poland
content: A complex engineering intervention secured slopes at risk of landslides in the Małopolska region, Poland. It ensures the continued operation of road transport in areas affected by increasing intense rainfall due to climate change.

title: National consultant – Development of localization model (Phnom Penh, Cambodia)
content: The purpose of this consultancy is to facilitate the development and documentation of a country (i.e., PPC)-specific Localization Model, capturing how localization has been defined, operationalized, and institutionalized through the partnership.

title: NOAA strengthens commitment to world-class tsunami monitoring and detection in Pacific
content: An agreement between NOAA, the Alaska Mesonet and the seismic network operated by the University of Alaska-Fairbanks Alaska Earthquake Center (UAF AEC) is funding nine AEC-owned seismic stations that support near-real-time tsunami threat information.

title: Strengthening cholera control through simulation-based training with Zambia’s water utilities
content: Organising preparedness training for water utilities in relation to cholera is a critical step in preventing and reducing outbreaks. It is essential because cholera is waterborne.

title: Where humanitarian action meets long-term water security
content: Rosine Kouame, an Ivorian water management expert with the Dutch Disaster Risk Reduction and Surge Support (DRRS) programme, led a team to address urgent local water needs while creating sustainable improvements for the community.

title: Clustering-based AI forecasts river water levels using just a few long records
content: Accurate forecasting via early warning systems is a highly useful technique for flood mitigation, agricultural irrigation, ecosystem and environmental sustainability, and numerous other applications.

title: Jeonbuk National University
content: Jeonbuk National University is one of ten Flagship Korean National Universities founded in 1947, located in Jeonju, South Korea.
